title: What to know about Scutellaria baicalensis before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Scutellaria baicalensis are fever reduction due to its flavonoid baicalein, which has been shown to inhibit the activity of the enzyme responsible for fever production. It is also used to alleviate inflammation, thanks to its anti-inflammatory flavonoids baicalein and baicalin. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids are effective in treating diarrhea by enhancing the integrity of the intestinal epithelial barrier. The flavone baicalein in Scutellaria baicalensis has been found to have anxiolytic properties, reducing anxiety by modulating the activity of GABA receptors. Its use also extends to treating cough, hepatitis, rheumatism, digestive issues, respiratory issues, and cardiovascular disease, all of which are alleviated by the plant's flavonoids, particularly baicalein and baicalin.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Scutellaria baicalensis are primarily exhibited through its antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins, providing protection against various pathogens. This plant also possesses antioxidant properties, which are mainly due to the flavonoids and phenolic acids, allowing it to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. Moreover, the anti-inflammatory properties of Scutellaria baicalensis are primarily associated with the flavonoids, which can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, contributing to its analgesic effect. Additionally, the plant's antipyretic properties are thought to be related to its ability to modulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, and its antiviral and antibacterial properties are attributed to the flavonoids and phenolic acids, respectively. Furthermore, the neuroprotective, antispasmodic, and anticancer properties of Scutellaria baicalensis are primarily associated with the flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can inhibit cell proliferation and induce apoptosis in cancer cells.
identification: Scutellaria baicalensis is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its taproot and woody rhizome, and stems that are 30-100 cm tall, with opposite, ovate-lanceolate leaves that are 3-8 cm long and 2-5 cm wide, and bear a cyme of blue or purple flowers in the axils of the upper leaves. It is native to Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea, and is widely cultivated in other parts of the region. The plant thrives in a variety of environments, from mountainous forests to grasslands, and can tolerate a range of soil conditions. In ideal conditions, it can grow up to 3 meters in height, with a spread of up to 1 meter. Scutellaria baicalensis is a hardy plant that can tolerate temperatures as low as -20°C and as high as 30°C.
cultivation: Scutellaria baicalensis is a perennial plant that exhibits a growth habit of spreading rhizomes and dense clusters of stems, typically reaching 1-3 meters in height and spread. It thrives in well-drained soils with a pH range of 6.0-7.0, and requires full sun to partial shade with moderate to high water levels, although it can tolerate drought for short periods. To promote optimal growth, Scutellaria baicalensis benefits from regular pruning to control stem height and encourage branching, as well as protection from extreme temperatures and humidity fluctuations. Soil fertility is not a critical factor, but regular fertilization with an organic matter-rich compost can enhance its growth and medicinal properties. Cultivating Scutellaria baicalensis in a greenhouse or controlled environment can help maintain a consistent temperature and humidity level, but it can also be grown outdoors in temperate climates with proper care.
history: Scutellaria baicalensis is a plant species native to China and Mongolia that has been used in traditional medicine for over 2,000 years. Its roots, also known as Huangqi, have been employed to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their purported anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Scutellaria baicalensis is believed to balance the body's yin and yang energies and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects. The plant's active compounds, including baicalein and baicalin, have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities in vitro and in vivo studies. Scutellaria baicalensis has been used in various traditional Chinese medicine formulas, such as Huangqi Tang and Huangqi Jianzhong Tang, to treat a range of health conditions.

constituents.name: Wogonin
constituents.description: Wogonin is a flavone constituent of Scutellaria baicalensis, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Baicalin
constituents.description: Baicalin is a flavonoid glycoside constituent of Scutellaria baicalensis with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Baicalein
constituents.description: Baicalein is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties.

constituents.name: Scutellarin
constituents.description: A flavone glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and the ability to inhibit platelet aggregation and suppress the proliferation of cancer cells.

constituents.name: Wogonoside
constituents.description: A flavone glycoside constituent of Scutellaria baicalensis, known for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids in Scutellaria baicalensis are a type of compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Scutellarein
constituents.description: Scutellarein is a flavone glycoside with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Oroxylin A
constituents.description: Oroxylin A is a flavone glycoside with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins from Scutellaria baicalensis are triterpenoid glycosides with hemolytic, foaming, and surface-active properties

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: A type of flavonoid found in Scutellaria baicalensis, including baicalin and wogonoside, which are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potentially hepatoprotective properties.

constituents.name: Orobol
constituents.description: Orobol is a flavonoid compound with diuretic and antispasmodic properties.

constituents.name: Vitexin
constituents.description: Vitexin is a flavonoid glycoside with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potentially neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-proliferative properties

constituents.name: Isorhapontigenin
constituents.description: Isorhapontigenin is a flavonoid that exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Baicalein and baicalin, two triterpenoids found in Scutellaria baicalensis, are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Oroxylin B
constituents.description: Oroxylin B is a flavonoid constituent of Scutellaria baicalensis, possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a type of lignan with a bitter, sweet, or astringent taste, and are known to be anticoagulant, anti-inflammatory, and have antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Luteolin
constituents.description: A flavonoid compound found in Scutellaria baicalensis, possessing antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Scutelloside
constituents.description: Scutelloside is a type of iridoid glycoside found in Scutellaria baicalensis, with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties

constituents.name: 3,4-Dihydroxyflavone
constituents.description: 3,4-Dihydroxyflavone is a flavonoid constituent of Scutellaria baicalensis, displaying antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Scutellaria baicalensis contains baicalein, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation and lead to abdominal pain.
Hypersensitivity to Scutellaria baicalensis constituents results in skin symptoms such as rash, itching, and swelling.
High doses of Scutellaria baicalensis may induce anxiety due to its ginsenoside content, which can interact with the neurotransmitter serotonin.
Scutellaria baicalensis can increase risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties.
Scutellaria baicalensis extracts induce cough in some individuals.
Experiencing a sudden loss of balance and equilibrium.
Increased mitochondrial dysfunction leads to reduced cellular energy production.
Scutellaria baicalensis contains baicalein, which causes gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and vomiting, in some individuals.
Scutellaria baicalensis causes a decrease in blood pressure
Scutellaria baicalensis contains baicalein, which causes hepatotoxicity and liver damage in high doses or with prolonged use.

uses_list.description: This herb is used for its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Extracts of this plant have been shown to possess hepatoprotective properties, which help to protect the liver from damage and promote liver cell regeneration. Its flavonoids and other compounds may help to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, making it a potential treatment for hepatitis.

This herb has been used to reduce fever due to its antipyretic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and other compounds. These compounds help to reduce body temperature by inhibiting the production of heat shock proteins and other pyrogens.

The flavonoids and other compounds in this herb may help to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the production of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) and other vasoconstrictors, which contribute to hypertension. Its antioxidant properties may also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels.

This herb has been used to treat diarrhea due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and eliminate pathogens in the gut. Its flavonoids and other compounds may also help to regulate bowel movements and improve gut health.

The flavonoids and other compounds in this herb may help to regulate blood sugar levels by improving insulin sensitivity and reducing inflammation in the pancreas. Its antioxidant properties may also help to reduce oxidative stress and prevent complications associated with diabetes.

This herb has been used to treat migraine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimigraine properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the head and neck. Its flavonoids and other compounds may also help to regulate blood vessel function and reduce stress.

The flavonoids and other compounds in this herb may help to reduce anxiety by regulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine. Its antioxidant properties may also help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

This herb has been used to treat gastritis due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and eliminate pathogens in the stomach. Its flavonoids and other compounds may also help to regulate gastric acid production and improve gut health.

The flavonoids and other compounds in this herb may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in joints by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Its antioxidant properties may also help to reduce oxidative stress and prevent joint damage.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Scutellaria baicalensis is used to treat various conditions, including hepatitis, a condition where the liver is inflamed due to viral infections, which is often treated with this plant's flavonoids. This plant's anti-inflammatory properties are also utilized to alleviate migraines, a severe headache disorder often triggered by stress. Additionally, its antimicrobial and antiviral properties make it effective in treating gastroenteritis, a diarrheal illness caused by bacterial or viral infections. Furthermore, studies have shown that the flavonoids present in Scutellaria baicalensis can help regulate blood pressure in patients suffering from hypertension, while its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties can help reduce fever. The plant's antioxidant properties also contribute to its role in cancer prevention, and it is also used to treat influenza, diarrhea, arthritis, anxiety, inflammatory bowel disease, gastritis, bacterial infections, diabetes, gastroesophageal reflux disease, depression, migraines, rheumatoid arthritis, and dyspepsia.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Scutellaria baicalensis is used to treat inflammation, a response to injury or infection, by reducing oxidative stress and modulating immune response. Baicalin, a flavonoid glycoside extracted from Scutellaria baicalensis, has been shown to alleviate symptoms of hepatitis, a viral infection of the liver, by inhibiting the replication of the virus. Additionally, the plant is used to treat diarrhea, a symptom of gastrointestinal disorders, by reducing inflammation and modulating the gut microbiome, and fever, a symptom of infections, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Other conditions treated with Scutellaria baicalensis include diabetes, cough, rheumatism, asthma, and cancer prevention, all of which are associated with the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is also used to treat a range of other conditions, including migraines, influenza, high blood pressure, anxiety, cardiovascular disease, depression, gastrointestinal issues, dyspepsia, gastroenteritis, pneumonia, and arthritis.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Scutellaria baicalensis is used to treat various health conditions. It is widely used to alleviate inflammation, a common symptom associated with several diseases, and has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also effective in treating hepatitis, a liver condition, as well as fever, which is often a symptom of various infections, including influenza and malaria. Baicalin, a flavonoid derived from Scutellaria baicalensis, has been found to have antitumor properties, making it a potential treatment for cancer, while its vasodilatory effects also make it useful in managing hypertension. Additionally, it is used to treat a range of other conditions, including diarrhea, diabetes, rheumatism, cough, asthma, gastroenteritis, dyspepsia, atherosclerosis, arthritis, menstrual pain, migraine, and cardiovascular disease, and is also being researched for its potential to treat neurodegenerative disease.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Scutellaria baicalensis is used to treat various health conditions, including hepatitis, and its antiviral properties have been effective in combating this liver disease. Additionally, the plant's cooling effect makes it an ideal remedy for fever, as well as inflammation, which is often associated with conditions such as gastritis. It also has a calming effect on the nervous system, making it useful in treating migraine and diarrhea, while also exhibiting antidiabetic properties that help manage diabetes. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties make it effective in treating rheumatism and arthritis, and it also helps in managing hypertension, asthma, anxiety, and dyspepsia, as well as relieving symptoms of influenza and skin infections. The plant also exhibits anticancer properties, and its antispasmodic and expectorant properties make it useful in treating gastroenteritis, cough, epilepsy, and respiratory issues, such as respiratory issues, like bronchitis.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Scutellaria baicalensis is used to treat various health conditions. It is often employed to alleviate inflammation, which can lead to other issues such as hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to help manage diabetes and anxiety, as well as reduce the severity of hepatitis. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties make it useful in treating migraines, fever, and gastritis, while also providing potential benefits in the treatment of cancer and arthritis. Furthermore, it may also help alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, gastroenteritis, asthma, epilepsy, atherosclerosis, rheumatism, migraines, gout, and cough.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its antioxidant effects. These compounds can neutralize free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and protect cells from damage, thereby maintaining overall health and well-being.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties. They can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of microorganisms, including bacteria, fungi, and viruses. This property makes it useful for treating infections and preventing the growth of pathogenic microorganisms.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to possess anticancer properties. They can inhibit the growth of cancer cells, induce apoptosis, and prevent the formation of new blood vessels that supply cancer cells with nutrients, thereby slowing down or preventing cancer progression.

The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been found to possess neuroprotective properties. They can protect neurons from damage, reduce inflammation, and improve cognitive function, thereby potentially alleviating conditions such as Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to possess antiviral properties. They can inhibit the replication of viruses, reduce the severity of viral infections, and prevent the transmission of viruses, thereby making it useful for treating viral infections.

The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been found to possess cardiovascular benefits. They can lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, prevent the formation of blood clots, and improve overall cardiovascular health, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases.

The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to possess antibacterial properties. They can inhibit the growth of bacteria, reduce the severity of bacterial infections, and prevent the transmission of bacteria, thereby making it useful for treating bacterial infections.

The plant's bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been found to possess immunomodulatory properties. They can stimulate the immune system, increase the production of antibodies, and enhance the activity of immune cells, thereby potentially alleviating conditions such as autoimmune diseases and immunodeficiency disorders.

The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, have been found to possess high antioxidant capacity. They can neutralize free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and protect cells from damage, thereby maintaining overall health and well-being.

benefits_description: Scutellaria baicalensis antioxidant effects are primarily attributed to the flavonoids present in the plant, which scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. The anti-inflammatory action of Scutellaria baicalensis is due to the inhibitory effect of baicalein on the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, resulting in the reduction of inflammation and alleviation of symptoms associated with various diseases. Scutellaria baicalensis antimicrobial properties, including antibacterial and antifungal activities, are responsible for its ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms and prevent infections. The anticancer potential of Scutellaria baicalensis is associated with its ability to induce apoptosis and inhibit cell proliferation in cancer cells, as well as its ability to prevent the formation of new blood vessels that feed cancer cells. Additionally, Scutellaria baicalensis neuroprotective benefits, antiviral activity, cardiovascular support, antibacterial properties, immunomodulatory effects, and antioxidant capacity have been observed in various studies, underscoring its potential as a therapeutic agent for the prevention and treatment of various diseases.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Scutellaria baicalensis improves blood flow by activating nitric oxide synthase, leading to the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator. Reducing blood pressure is achieved through the inhibition of the renin-angiotensin system and the suppression of aldosterone secretion. The flavonoids present in Scutellaria baicalensis, particularly baicalein, enhance vasodilation by stimulating the release of endothelium-dependent relaxing factors. Supporting cardiac function is attributed to the antioxidant properties of the plant, which protect the heart from oxidative stress and damage. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties prevent atherosclerosis formation, decrease blood clots, relax blood vessels, stabilize platelet aggregation, increase oxygen delivery, reduce inflammation response, and fight against the progression of cardiovascular diseases.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Scutellaria baicalensis reduces inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its ability to soothe diarrhea and prevent ulcers in the digestive system. Improving digestion is also a key benefit of this plant, attributed to its antioxidant properties that protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage. Relieving nausea is another significant advantage, primarily due to its antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial overgrowth in the gut. Enhancing gut health is facilitated by the plant's ability to reduce gut irritation and promote a balanced gut microbiome, ultimately leading to the cure of colitis. By protecting the gut lining and reducing gut irritation, Scutellaria baicalensis also fights bacterial overgrowth and promotes overall gut health.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Scutellaria baicalensis regulates adrenal function by exerting anti-inflammatory properties, thereby reducing oxidative stress and promoting adrenal gland health. The plant supports thyroid function by modulating the expression of thyroid hormone receptors and enhancing the conversion of T4 to T3, ultimately alleviating thyroid-related disorders. Scutellaria baicalensis balances insulin levels by inhibiting the activity of alpha-glucosidase and alpha-amylase enzymes, thus reducing glucose absorption and absorption of sugar molecules in the intestines. This herb reduces cortisol levels by blocking the activity of 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1, an enzyme involved in cortisol synthesis, thereby mitigating stress and anxiety-related symptoms. The medicinal properties of Scutellaria baicalensis also enhance metabolism by stimulating the expression of genes involved in glucose and lipid metabolism, activate the pancreas by inducing the production of pancreatic digestive enzymes, stimulate the pituitary gland by increasing the production of growth hormone and thyroid-stimulating hormone, decrease androgens by inhibiting the activity of 17-alpha-hydroxylase, promote the ovaries by regulating the expression of estrogen receptors, and modulate estrogen levels by inhibiting the activity of aromatase enzyme.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Scutellaria baicalensis protects skin from damage caused by UV radiation and environmental stress through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that can lead to cell damage. The anti-inflammatory properties of Scutellaria baicalensis reduce inflammation, a key factor in various skin conditions, such as acne and rosacea. Its soothing effects on irritated skin are due to the presence of flavonoids, which calm the skin and reduce redness. Additionally, the elasticity of skin is improved by the plant's ability to stimulate collagen production, a process that helps to maintain skin firmness and tone. Scutellaria baicalensis also exhibits antimicrobial and cooling properties, allowing it to fight acne, heal wounds, enhance moisturizing, reduce scarring, cool burns, and antioxidantize skin, thereby maintaining skin health and preventing various skin problems.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Scutellaria baicalensis enhances lymph flow by increasing the production of lymphatic vessels and promoting the clearance of metabolic waste products. This plant supports lymph node health by exerting its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce the swelling and inflammation associated with lymph node disorders. Scutellaria baicalensis also boosts immune function by stimulating the production of cytokines, which coordinate the immune response to pathogens. The plant's antioxidant properties reduce inflammation levels by neutralizing free radicals and promoting the repair of damaged tissues, and it improves detoxification process by inducing the expression of detoxification enzymes. Scutellaria baicalensis stimulates lymphatic drainage, fights bacterial infections with its antimicrobial properties, suppresses tumor growth by inhibiting the proliferation of cancer cells, and maintains healthy lipids by modulating the expression of genes involved in lipid metabolism, which activates antioxidant response.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Scutellaria baicalensis relieves pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and neurotransmitters responsible for pain transmission. Reducing inflammation is also a key aspect of its medicinal properties, as Scutellaria baicalensis exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that counteract the effects of free radicals and inflammatory cytokines. Soothing arthritis is another benefit of Scutellaria baicalensis, due to its ability to modulate the immune response and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory mediators. Easing tension and preventing damage are also attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect cells and tissues from oxidative stress and damage. By exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, Scutellaria baicalensis heals tendons, calms muscles, stabilizes joints, and regenerates cartilage, ultimately promoting musculoskeletal health.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Scutellaria baicalensis reduces stress by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which protect the nervous system from oxidative damage and inflammation. The antimicrobial properties of Scutellaria baicalensis alleviate anxiety by preventing the growth of pathogens that can contribute to anxiety disorders. Additionally, its flavonoid content improves mood by regulating neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, while also enhancing focus through its neuroprotective and vasodilatory effects. The adaptogenic properties of Scutellaria baicalensis regulate emotions by promoting balance and stability in the body, relieve fatigue by improving energy metabolism, and increase alertness by stimulating the central nervous system. Furthermore, Scutellaria baicalensis prevents depression by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and soothes nerves by reducing the excitability of neurons, ultimately supporting brain health by protecting against age-related cognitive decline.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Scutellaria baicalensis regulates hormonal balance by exerting estrogen-modulating effects due to its phytoestrogenic properties, allowing for a stable reproductive environment. The plant supports ovulation by stimulating the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone, which in turn regulates the menstrual cycle. Its anti-inflammatory properties improve fertility by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the reproductive system, promoting a healthy environment for conception. Scutellaria baicalensis enhances reproductive function by modulating the expression of genes involved in the menstrual cycle, leading to regular and synchronized ovulation. By reducing menstrual cramping and regulating menstrual flow, improving endometrial thickness and receptivity, enhancing sperm quality through antioxidant activity, supporting embryonic development with its anti-apoptotic properties, preventing miscarriage through its anti-inflammatory effects, and promoting menstrual regularity with its estrogen-modulating effects, Scutellaria baicalensis plays a vital role in maintaining reproductive health.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Scutellaria baicalensis reduces inflammation by exerting anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate respiratory issues. Fights bacterial infections through its antimicrobial properties, making it effective against respiratory pathogens. It relaxes airways due to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content, facilitating easier breathing. This plant prevents mucus buildup by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thereby reducing respiratory secretions. Scutellaria baicalensis stimulates coughing through its expectorant properties, aiding in the removal of mucus and debris from the respiratory tract, while lowering allergy symptoms by inhibiting the release of histamine, fighting viral infections with its antiviral properties, soothing the respiratory tract with its emollient properties, easing congestion by thinning mucus, and improving breathing by enhancing lung function.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Scutellaria baicalensis prevents kidney damage by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which protect kidney cells from oxidative stress and inflammation. It reduces urine frequency by its diuretic effect, which is attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. Additionally, Scutellaria baicalensis relieves bladder pain by its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pain-causing mediators. The plant also treats urinary tract infections by its antimicrobial properties, including baicalein and baicalin, which inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria. Furthermore, Scutellaria baicalensis protects bladder health, decreases urine bacteria, heals kidney stones, relaxes urinary muscles, soothes irritated bladder, and prevents prostate enlargement by its flavonoid and phenolic acid content, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

properties_list.description: Scutellaria baicalensis has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various bacteria, including Escherichia coli, Staphylococcus aureus, and Bacillus subtilis, through its flavonoid and phenolic acid constituents.

The plant contains flavonoids, such as baicalein and baicalin, which have been demonstrated to possess antioxidant activity, scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress in various cell types.

Scutellaria baicalensis has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, mediated by its flavonoids, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, such as COX-2 and iNOS, in various cell types.

The plant has been traditionally used as an analgesic, and studies have shown that its flavonoids, particularly baicalein, exhibit analgesic activity by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and modulating pain-related neurotransmitters.

Scutellaria baicalensis has been found to provide pain relief through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the modulation of pain-related neurotransmitters and the inhibition of pro-inflammatory mediators.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat spasmodic conditions, such as muscle cramps and spasms, and studies have shown that its flavonoids exhibit antispasmodic activity by relaxing smooth muscle and inhibiting the release of acetylcholine.

Scutellaria baicalensis has been found to exhibit antiviral activity against various viruses, including herpes simplex virus and influenza virus, through its flavonoids, which can inhibit viral replication and reduce the severity of viral infections.

The plant has been found to possess cardioprotective properties, which can be attributed to its flavonoids, which can reduce oxidative stress, improve lipid profiles, and inhibit the development of atherosclerosis.

Scutellaria baicalensis has been found to possess neuroprotective properties, which can be attributed to its flavonoids, which can reduce oxidative stress, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, and promote neuronal survival in various neurological disorders.

The plant has been found to possess hepatoprotective properties, which can be attributed to its flavonoids, which can reduce oxidative stress, improve liver function, and inhibit the development of liver fibrosis in various liver diseases.

parts_description: The most used parts of Scutellaria baicalensis for medicinal purposes are the roots and leaves. The roots are rich in baicalin, a flavonoid glycoside, and wogonoside, a flavonoid, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties. The leaves, on the other hand, are primarily composed of baicalein, a flavonoid, and wogonin, a flavone, which possess anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and anti-cancer properties. Both the roots and leaves have been traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their ability to modulate the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. The unique combination of bioactive compounds in the roots and leaves makes Scutellaria baicalensis a valuable medicinal plant with diverse therapeutic applications.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain baicalein, baicalin, and wogonin as major bioactive compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, primarily contributing to its analgesic, anti-edematous, and anti-cancer health benefits, as well as its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in baicalein, baicalin, and wogonin, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, leading to its application in treating fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to prevent cancer and cardiovascular diseases, due to its ability to scavenge free radicals and modulate immune responses.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat fever, inflammation, and anxiety disorders, as well as to prevent and treat infections such as pneumonia and sepsis.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria baicalensis capsule is used to treat inflammation, stress, fever, anxiety, fatigue, headache, cough, rheumatism, sore throat and bleeding. This preparation is widely used and considered a common remedy in many cultures. The most common effect reported is its ability to reduce inflammation and fever. It is typically made from the roots, roots, leaves and flowers of the Scutellaria baicalensis plant. The process of extracting these compounds and creating the capsule is moderately complex, requiring careful handling and processing to ensure the desired therapeutic properties are preserved.
preparation_recipe: Dry the roots of Scutellaria baicalensis in a well-ventilated area to remove excess moisture.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered roots with dried leaves of Scutellaria baicalensis and mix well.
Add dried flowers of Scutellaria baicalensis to the mixture and grind the blend into a uniform powder.
Fill gelatin capsules with the Scutellaria baicalensis powder and seal them properly to create herbal capsules.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat fever, inflammation, and liver and gallbladder issues, and to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria baicalensis tincture is a herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including inflammation, hepatitis, fever, eczema, gastroenteritis, bronchitis, pneumonia, cough, migraine, diarrhea, laryngitis, and sinusitis. It is a common preparation in traditional Chinese medicine and has been used for centuries to treat these conditions. The preparation is known to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which contribute to its efficacy in treating the mentioned conditions. Scutellaria baicalensis tincture is typically made from the roots, flowers, and other parts of the Scutellaria baicalensis plant, which are combined in various proportions to create the final product. The process of making the tincture is moderately difficult and requires careful measurement and attention to detail to ensure the desired potency and quality. As a result, the tincture is widely available in health food stores and online, and is often used by individuals seeking a natural alternative to pharmaceutical treatments.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean Scutellaria baicalensis roots and flowers, ensuring they are free from debris and contaminants.
Combine the cleaned Scutellaria baicalensis roots and flowers in a clean glass container.
Cover the container with a lid and allow it to steep in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin, for an extended period, typically 2-6 weeks.
Strain the solvent from the container using cheesecloth or a coffee filter, separating the liquid from the solids.
Store the resulting herbal tincture in a clean glass bottle, protecting it from light and heat, and label it accordingly.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat fever, inflammation, and infections, and to improve cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria baicalensis tea is a traditional herbal preparation used to treat hypertension, gastritis, diabetes, influenza, anxiety, cough, and fever. This tea is widely used due to its common application in traditional medicine. Its diuretic effect is the most common effect reported. The preparation is made from the roots, leaves, and flowers of the Scutellaria baicalensis plant. The tea is relatively easy to prepare by steeping the plant material in hot water.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried Scutellaria baicalensis roots and leaves from a trusted source.
Wash the roots and leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Combine the roots and leaves in a pot and add enough water to cover the plant material.
Bring the water to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the roots and leaves, serving the Scutellaria baicalensis herbal tea hot or cold as desired.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used topically to treat fever, inflammation, and infections, and also to promote wound healing, reduce pain and swelling, and improve skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria baicalensis salve is a topical preparation derived from the roots and leaves of Scutellaria baicalensis, a plant native to Asia. It's used to treat burns, wounds, bruises, insect bites, skin infections, allergic reactions, minor cuts, and eczema. This salve is not commonly used due to its relatively unknown effects and the difficulty of obtaining the necessary plant material. Its uncommon effect on inflammation and skin conditions is attributed to the presence of baicalein and baicalin, two flavonoids with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The salve is made by extracting these compounds from the roots and leaves, a process that's challenging and time-consuming, requiring skilled craftsmen and specialized equipment.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Scutellaria baicalensis roots and leaves, and dry them thoroughly to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried roots and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Combine the ground roots and leaves powder with a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat, usually between 100°F to 150°F, for several hours to infuse the carrier oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discard the solids, and let the salve cool and solidify before use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections due to its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Scutellaria baicalensis suppository is a rare herbal preparation used to treat various conditions, including gastroenteritis, cystitis, dysentery, prostatitis, vaginitis and diarrhea. It's not commonly used due to its limited availability and lack of popularity. This suppository has an uncommon effect, as it's often used to treat conditions that are not typically associated with suppositories. The preparation is made from Scutellaria baicalensis roots, flowers and possibly other parts of the plant. The process of making Scutellaria baicalensis suppository is complex and time-consuming, requiring extensive knowledge of herbalism and pharmacology to produce a safe and effective product.
preparation_recipe: Harvest and clean Scutellaria baicalensis roots and flowers, and dry them in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground root powder with a small amount of natural binder, such as beeswax or vegetable oil, to create a uniform mixture.
Add a small amount of dried Scutellaria baicalensis flowers to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Fill the mixture into suppository molds and allow them to set at room temperature before use.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Scutellaria baicalensis are abdominal pain, which can range from mild to severe, and allergic reaction, a potentially life-threatening condition that requires immediate medical attention. In some cases, anxiety may develop due to the plant's stimulatory effects on the nervous system. Additionally, bleeding can occur, particularly in individuals taking anticoagulant medications. Furthermore, cough, dizziness, and fatigue are common side effects associated with the plant's stimulatory properties. Improper use of Scutellaria baicalensis can also cause gastrointestinal disturbance, hypotension, and potentially severe liver damage.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Not recommended during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to potential effects on fetal or infant development.
Scutellaria baicalensis may lower blood sugar levels and interact with diabetes medications, requiring regular monitoring of blood sugar levels.
Scutellaria baicalensis may cause uterine contractions and inhibit milk production.
High doses of Scutellaria baicalensis can cause gastrointestinal upset and interact with anticoagulant medications.
Scutellaria baicalensis may interact with diabetes medications such as repaglinide.
Scutellaria baicalensis contains baicalein, which has vasodilatory effects that decrease peripheral resistance and lower blood pressure.
Scutellaria baicalensis may increase the risk of bleeding when used with anticoagulant medications such as warfarin, aspirin, and clopidogrel.
Scutellaria baicalensis may worsen liver conditions in individuals with existing liver disease.
Concomitant use with sedatives and antidepressants increases the risk of serotonin syndrome and other adverse effects.
Scutellaria baicalensis causes allergic reactions in some individuals.

intro_study: According to a study published by "The Journal of pharmacy and pharmacology", Scutellaria baicalensis has shown significant effects on the treatment of various diseases, especially hepatitis, diarrhoea, vomiting, and high blood pressure. It exhibits a wide range of pharmacological activities, including effects on the nervous system, immune system, and liver protection. The compounds isolated from Scutellaria baicalensis also possess antitumour, antibacterial, antiviral, and antioxidant effects.

According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Scutellaria baicalensis exhibits a range of beneficial properties, including effective antioxidant activity that can neutralize free radicals and moderate reducing power and chelating activity. Additionally, the extract from Scutellaria baicalensis demonstrates good antimicrobial potential, particularly in inhibiting the growth of certain strains of bacteria and fungi, such as Trichophyton tonsurans. Furthermore, the study found that Scutellaria baicalensis lacks biotoxicity, as evidenced by its inability to cause lethality in the brine shrimp bioassay.

According to a study published by "Planta medica", the extract from the root of Scutellaria baicalensis showed a preferential effect on the synthesis of collagen and total protein, indicating its potential in promoting tissue health. The flavonoids present in Scutellaria baicalensis, particularly baicalein and baicalin, were found to significantly inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules such as IL-1 beta and PGE2, suggesting their anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, these flavonoids were shown to augment the cellular activity of fibroblasts, leading to enhanced synthesis of collagen and total protein, which can be beneficial for wound healing and tissue repair.

According to a study published by "Wiadomosci lekarskie (Warsaw, Poland : 1960)", Scutellaria baicalensis has been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antithrombotic, antibacterial, and antiviral properties due to its flavonoid constituents. These properties suggest that it may help prevent or mitigate the factors contributing to atherosclerosis, such as oxidant stress and endothelium dysfunction. The potential use of Scutellaria baicalensis in the primary and secondary prevention of atherosclerosis may make it a valuable tool in managing this complex disease.

According to a study published by "Ceska a Slovenska farmacie : casopis Ceske farmaceuticke spolecnosti a Slovenske farmaceuticke spolecnosti", Scutellaria baicalensis has been found to possess various beneficial properties, including antioxidant, antiviral, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, antiallergic, and sedative effects. These properties support its traditional use in medicine and suggest that it may have more utilizable therapeutic effects, particularly in the treatment of certain health conditions. Furthermore, the flavonoid baicalein, which is derived from Scutellaria baicalensis, has shown promise as a prospective medicine for treating certain types of cancer.

According to a study published by "Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica", regular cutting seedling of Scutellaria baicalensis has shown to improve not only the yield of medicinal materials but also the active ingredient contents. The contents of flavonoids, baicalin, and other active ingredients such as wogonoside, baicalein, and wogonin in the plant have been enhanced to a significant extent, reaching up to 18.52% and 15.13% respectively in roots. This cultivation technique has the potential to promote the wider range of Scutellaria baicalensis cultivation and rational utilization of natural resources.

According to a study published by "Science bulletin", Scutellaria baicalensis has been reported to have various pharmacological functions, including anti-cancer, hepatoprotection, antibacterial and antiviral, antioxidant, anticonvulsant and neuroprotective effects. This medicinal plant has been widely used to treat various health conditions, such as diarrhea, dysentery, hypertension, hemorrhaging, insomnia, inflammation and respiratory infections. The bioactive compounds extracted from its root, particularly flavones like baicalin and baicalein, have been found to possess these beneficial properties.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Scutellaria baicalensis are multifaceted, with significant antimicrobial activity against a range of pathogens, primarily attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant exhibits potent antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress, while its anti-inflammatory effects are mediated by the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to its analgesic properties by reducing pain perception. Furthermore, Scutellaria baicalensis has been found to possess antispasmodic effects, alleviating muscle spasms and cramps, and its antiviral activity has been demonstrated against certain viral infections. Additionally, the cardioprotective effects of Scutellaria baicalensis are thought to be due to the modulation of cardiovascular risk factors, including lipid profiles and blood pressure, which also contribute to its neuroprotective properties by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. Lastly, Scutellaria baicalensis has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, protecting the liver from damage caused by toxic substances and promoting its natural detoxification processes, while its pain relief effects are thought to be a result of its combined analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Scutellaria baicalensis are wogonin, baicalin, baicalein, scutellarin, and wogonoside, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. Baicalin and baicalein have been shown to inhibit the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and iNOS, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Wogonin has been found to exhibit potent antiviral activity against herpes simplex virus and HIV, while scutellarin has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Wogonoside has been identified as a flavone glycoside with potential anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. These constituents are responsible for the therapeutic properties of Scutellaria baicalensis, including its use in traditional medicine for treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Scutellaria baicalensis for medicinal purposes are capsules, which contain a standardized extract of baicalein and baicalin to treat inflammation and improve cognitive function. The plant is also processed into a tincture, a concentrated liquid that is used topically to reduce pain and inflammation, and internally to treat gastrointestinal issues and as an antiviral agent. As a tea, Scutellaria baicalensis is consumed to alleviate fever and digestive problems, and it is also used as a salve to treat skin conditions and wounds. The root of the plant is sometimes prepared as a suppository to treat hemorrhoids and other anal conditions. Additionally, it has been used to treat various other ailments, including anxiety and depression.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Scutellaria baicalensis medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional to determine the safe dosage and potential interactions with other medications. Monitoring blood sugar levels is also crucial, as baicalin in the plant may have a hypoglycemic effect. Avoid using Scutellaria baicalensis during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions and affect fetal development. Be cautious with high doses, as they may lead to adverse effects such as digestive issues and allergic reactions, and interact with diabetes medications to prevent hypoglycemia. Additionally, individuals with liver conditions or those taking blood thinners, sedatives, or antidepressants should exercise caution, as Scutellaria baicalensis may exacerbate liver conditions, lower blood pressure, and interact with these medications.
intro_description: Scutellaria baicalensis, commonly known as Baikal skullcap, is a herb widely used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and viral infections, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The main health benefits of this herb include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, which contribute to its therapeutic potential. The therapeutic properties of Scutellaria baicalensis include its hepatoprotective, anti-diabetic, and anti-cancer activities, which are attributed to its ability to modulate various biological pathways. The bioactive compounds responsible for these effects include flavonoids, such as baicalein and baicalin, which have been identified as the primary active constituents of this herb. In traditional medicine, Scutellaria baicalensis is often prepared as a tea or capsule, and its extract is used to treat a range of health conditions, including viral hepatitis and type 2 diabetes.
